body:
Title: CRA Form SK479 2025 Saskatchewan credit.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5008-tc/5008-tc-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5008-tc/5008-tc-25e.pdf

SK479 numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 120000
Value: 300
Value: 400
Value: 0.50
Value: 10000

CRA Form SK479 2025 calculates the Saskatchewan credit. Lines 1 to 6 calculate adjusted family income by adding UCCB and RDSP repayments to net income, subtracting UCCB and RDSP income, flooring negative amounts at zero, and adding taxpayer and spouse or common-law partner amounts. The active families benefit is available if adjusted family income is $120,000 or less; otherwise line 8 is zero. The active families benefit worksheet column 5 caps eligible activity amounts at $300 per child unless the child is eligible for the disability tax credit, in which case the cap is $400. Line 7 adds the column 5 amounts, and line 8/59800 enters line 7 when adjusted family income does not exceed $120,000. Line 9 multiplies eligible medical expenses for fertility treatment by 50% and caps the fertility treatment tax credit at $10,000. Line 10 adds line 8 and line 9 and enters the Saskatchewan credit on T1 line 47900.
heading: CRA Form SK479 2025 Saskatchewan Credit
